summary: Provision a brand-new Amazon Keyspaces (Cassandra-compatible) keyspace. No Keyspaces estate exists yet, so this is a first stand-up an engineer reviews before it applies.

operations.title: Provision a new Keyspaces keyspace
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Cassandra-compatible keyspace; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a keyspace as a complete specification: name and replication strategy. Tables (with their own capacity and TTL settings) are added afterward as an engineer follow-up. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: keyspace_name
params.label: Keyspace name
params.help: Letters, numbers and underscores, starting with a letter. Names the keyspace queries select.

params.name: replication_strategy
params.label: Replication strategy
params.help: MULTI_REGION replicates every table in this keyspace to other Regions; the region list is an engineer decision.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Keyspace name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this keyspace holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this keyspace.

decisions: Multi-region replication's region list is an engineer decision — this form provisions the common single-region case
Tables, with their own capacity mode and TTL settings, are an engineer follow-up after the keyspace exists
